models.generation_context: 7th-gen Mustang (S650), introduced for 2024 MY; carryover for 2026 with new EcoBoost special editions (Big Bend / Mojave) and minor pricing changes. Mustang remains the only mainstream V8 muscle car in the US after Camaro and Challenger discontinuations.
models.model_summary: Two-door rear-wheel-drive sports coupe (Fastback) and convertible offered with turbocharged 2.3L EcoBoost or naturally-aspirated 5.0L Coyote V8 (GT) powertrains. Convertible body available on EcoBoost, EcoBoost Premium, and GT Premium. Higher-output Dark Horse, Dark Horse SC, and GTD variants are sold as separate models on ford.com.

reliability.summary: No JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports numeric predicted-reliability for 2026 MY at research time. S650-gen Mustang (2024-2025) has shown some owner-reported electronics glitches with the SYNC 4 infotainment and digital cluster, but mechanically the Coyote V8 and 2.3L EcoBoost are mature designs.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Mustang as one of the few remaining affordable V8 RWD sports coupes, with sharp styling, an evocative exhaust on the GT, and a much-improved digital cockpit. Common critiques are an interior that still trails some rivals in materials, technology glitches noted by some testers, and limited rear-seat space.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners love how the Mustang looks, sounds, and drives, frequently praising the V8 acceleration and styling. Complaints center on touchscreen glitches, key-fob issues, and inconsistent dealer service. Edmunds expert rating 7.3/10.

notable_other: Selectable Drive Modes (Normal, Sport, Slippery, Drag, Track)

trims.notes: Base trim for Mustang. NHTSA has not rated the 2026 Mustang. IIHS tested 2015-2026 with incomplete rating (only one-side small overlap, no TSP). Sports coupe with 2+2 seating; rear is tight. 'Premium' trim of EcoBoost adds heated/cooled ActiveX seats, wireless charging, B&O audio.

changes: Same 2.3L EcoBoost powertrain, dimensions, and safety as base

trims.notes: EcoBoost Premium adds comfort/tech upgrades over base; shares all mechanicals.

changes: Soft-top convertible body style replaces Fastback hard roof
Curb weight rises ~270 lb for soft-top reinforcements
Trunk volume reduced to 11.4 cu ft (from 13.5)
0-60 time slightly slower (4.7 vs 4.5 sec) due to added weight

trims.notes: Convertible body style; soft-top with insulated headliner. Shares EcoBoost powertrain with Fastback.

changes: Adds wireless phone charging, memory seats

trims.notes: EcoBoost Premium Convertible price approximate; includes both convertible body and Premium equipment upgrades.

notable_other: 5.0L Coyote V8 (480 hp / 486 hp w/ Active Valve Performance Exhaust)
Selectable Drive Modes incl. Drag and Track

trims.notes: Base trim for V8 powertrain line — Mustang has two powertrain lines (EcoBoost and GT V8); each has its own base per multi-powertrain rule. EPA: 15/23/18 with manual; 16/24/19 with automatic (ID 49792). With Active Valve Performance Exhaust output rises to 486 hp.

notable_other: Available FX Package (Oxford White wheels, plaid inserts)

changes: FX Package optional (Oxford White wheels, plaid inserts)

trims.notes: GT Premium adds luxury comfort over base GT; same 480-hp 5.0L V8.

Sole convertible offering on GT V8 powertrain line; only GT body style available is the Premium convertible (no base GT convertible). Trim_family gt-convertible is singleton — atomic sole-trim rule does NOT apply because it shares powertrain line with GT Fastback.

models.notes: Mustang has two powertrain lines (EcoBoost 2.3L I4 and GT 5.0L V8), each with its own base trim (EcoBoost Fastback and GT Fastback). Convertible body offered on EcoBoost/EcoBoost Premium/GT Premium only. Dark Horse, Dark Horse SC, and GTD are split as separate models per perf-variant rule (each has dedicated ford.com page). New for 2026: special-edition Big Bend/Mojave EcoBoost packages. Sole-trim rule does not apply to gt-convertible trim_family (only one trim) because it shares the GT V8 powertrain line with gt-fastback (which is the base of that line). Images flagged needs_scraping; ford.com consumer pages do not expose direct CDN asset URLs to WebFetch.

models.generation_context: S650-platform Dark Horse, introduced for 2024 MY as the first Dark Horse Mustang since the original 1969 race-bred Mustangs; carryover for 2026 with the new top-tier Dark Horse SC variant joining the lineup as its own model.
models.model_summary: Track-focused 5.0L V8 Mustang with hand-finished engine internals, MagneRide dampers, Brembo brakes, and a 500-hp tuned Coyote V8. Fastback coupe only. Available in Dark Horse and Dark Horse Premium trims; manual or automatic.

reliability.summary: No JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports 2026 numeric predicted-reliability data published at research time. Shares Coyote V8 base architecture with GT but with bespoke hand-built components.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rate the Dark Horse as the most engaging modern Mustang, with sharper chassis tuning, the TREMEC manual option, and louder, more responsive 500-hp Coyote V8. Common critiques echo the standard Mustang's interior tech glitches and limited rear room.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds expert rating 7.0/10. Owner samples for 2026 MY too limited to summarize aggregate praise/complaints at research time.

notable_other: Hand-built 5.0L Coyote V8 with strengthened internals (500 hp)
TREMEC TR-3160 6-speed manual standard with rev matching

trims.notes: Base trim of Dark Horse model. EPA: 14/22/17 (manual ID 49796); 14/22/17 (auto ID 49795). Sole-trim-line model: only ICE V8 powertrain offered. Dark Horse Premium is a step-up trim (luxury/comfort upgrades, ~$10k more).

notable_other: Premium leather sport seats with Recaro option
Available Handling Package: 19x10.5 front / 19x11 rear wheels, Pirelli Trofeo RS tires, lighter aluminum strut tower brace, adjustable rear wing

changes: Adds heated steering wheel, memory driver seat
Available Handling Package adds Trofeo RS tires, wider wheels, adjustable wing

trims.notes: Dark Horse Premium adds creature comforts; powertrain and chassis unchanged from base.

models.notes: Mustang Dark Horse is a separate model from Mustang (own ford.com page). Two trims (Dark Horse / Dark Horse Premium). Coupe (Fastback) only — no convertible. 500-hp tuned Coyote V8 with TREMEC manual or 10-speed auto. EPA 14/22/17 across manual and auto.

models.generation_context: All-new for 2026 — first-ever Dark Horse SC variant, slotting between the Dark Horse and the limited GTD supercar. Uses a detuned version of the GTD's supercharged Predator V8. Available to order spring 2026, deliveries summer 2026.
models.model_summary: Track-bred Mustang with supercharged 5.2L Predator V8 (795 hp), 7-speed Tremec dual-clutch transmission, carbon-fiber driveshaft, and motorsport-grade chassis tuning. Available as standard Dark Horse SC and Track Pack Special Edition. Fastback coupe only.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too new/low-volume for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: Dark Horse SC is all-new for 2026 — too recent for individual JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports sampling. Brand-level: Ford scored 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (released Feb 2026), well below the 204 industry average and ranking near the bottom of the pack. CR 2026 ranked Ford 11th overall with brand score 48/100 — Ford's best showing in 15 years. Predator V8 architecture is shared with F-150 Raptor R and Mustang GTD (longer-running flagship engines).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); model is also brand-new for 2026.

professional_reviews.summary: Early press coverage frames the Dark Horse SC as the most accessible 795-hp supercharged Mustang ever, blending GT500-style firepower with Dark Horse-derived chassis hardware. Reviewers note the Track Pack Special Edition's carbon-ceramic brakes, carbon wheels, and active aero as serious track equipment for the price-point. Limited road-test data at research time given spring 2026 launch.

owner_reviews.summary: No owner-review aggregates available (model launches summer 2026).

notable_other: 5.2L supercharged Predator V8 (shared with Mustang GTD and F-150 Raptor R)
MagneRide adaptive damping (Dark Horse SC-specific calibration)
Available Track Pack Special Edition ($36,500): MagneRide retuned, 20-inch carbon wheels, Brembo carbon-ceramic brakes, carbon-fiber ducktail wing, 620 lb downforce at 180 mph

trims.notes: Sole-trim model (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). EPA fuel economy not yet published for Dark Horse SC 2026 — source set to brand model-browse page per spec §4 EPA-unavailable fallback. Track Pack Special Edition is a $36,500 option package per spec §6.5, not a separate trim. Production launches summer 2026.

models.notes: Dark Horse SC is a separate model from Mustang Dark Horse on ford.com. Sole-trim model with 795-hp supercharged Predator V8 and 7-speed dual-clutch. Production starts summer 2026; EPA has not yet published 2026 figures. Track Pack ($36,500) treated as package per §6.5 (no separate trim entry).

models.generation_context: First-ever production Mustang GTD, developed jointly with Multimatic and based on the IMSA Mustang GT3 race car. Limited-production homologation supercar; ongoing build through 2026 MY.
models.model_summary: Limited-production Mustang supercar with 5.2L supercharged Predator V8 (815 hp), rear-mounted 8-speed dual-clutch transaxle, pushrod inboard rear suspension, Brembo carbon-ceramic brakes, and street-legal homologation of Ford Performance's IMSA Mustang GT3 race car. Two-seat configuration with rear suspension viewing window. Allocation by application only.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (model too low-volume for individual VDS)
reliability.summary: Mustang GTD is a limited-production homologation supercar — too low-volume for individual JD Power VDS or Consumer Reports sampling. Brand-level: Ford scored 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204), ranking near the bottom. CR 2026 ranked Ford 11th overall with brand score 48/100 (Ford's best showing in 15 years).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July); limited-production halo car unlikely to receive individual APEAL coverage.

professional_reviews.summary: Press coverage frames the GTD as Ford's most extreme street-legal Mustang ever — a true homologation supercar with a rear transaxle, race-derived pushrod rear suspension visible through a polycarbonate trunk window, and 815 hp from the supercharged Predator V8. Track lap times have outpaced six-figure European exotics in early testing.

owner_reviews.summary: Limited-production halo car; no aggregate owner-review data available.

notable_other: Pushrod inboard rear suspension with adaptive spool valve dampers
Polycarbonate rear window viewable to suspension hardware
Brembo carbon-ceramic brakes (17-inch front rotors, 6-piston calipers)
Massive carbon-fiber active aerodynamics (DRS rear wing)
Magnesium wheels with Michelin Pilot Sport Cup 2 R tires
Allocation by application only — not sold through standard dealer channels

trims.notes: Sole-trim model (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). Categorized as sports-car body style per spec §5 (two-seat configuration, race-bred). NHTSA and IIHS have not tested this halo model. Allocation by application — not in standard dealer inventory. ADAS booleans all false (race-focused stripped interior).

models.notes: Mustang GTD is a separate model from Mustang on ford.com (own /performance/mustang-gtd/ page). Sole-trim halo supercar with rear transaxle layout. Two-seat configuration eliminates rear bench. Body style classified as sports-car (vs Mustang coupe) per its dedicated platform and homologation purpose.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Mustang Mach-E, introduced for 2021 MY; 2024+ revisions added 9.6 kW AC charging and updated software. For 2026 MY Ford has standardized NACS adapter compatibility with Tesla Supercharger access.
models.model_summary: All-electric compact crossover SUV in the Mustang family of performance vehicles. Standard- or Extended-range battery, RWD or AWD. Three trims (Select, Premium, GT) with the Mach-E Rally split as its own model. NACS-adapter Tesla Supercharger access enabled.

reliability.summary: No 2026 MY numeric reliability data published. Earlier Mach-E (2021-2024) had above-average warranty claims due to high-voltage battery contactor issues that prompted recalls; software fixes deployed.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Mach-E for its strong acceleration, well-tuned chassis, generous interior space, and now-broader DC fast-charge access via Tesla Superchargers (NACS adapter). Common critiques include the polarizing 15.5-inch portrait touchscreen interface, occasional software glitches, and middling charging speed (~150 kW peak) versus newer EV competitors.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the driving feel, instant torque, and modern interior. Common complaints involve software/over-the-air-update quirks, slower-than-rated DC fast charging in real world, and tech glitches with the touchscreen.

notable_other: Frunk (4.7 cu ft) front trunk for additional storage
NACS-adapter compatibility for Tesla Supercharger access (adapter included)

trims.notes: Base Mach-E trim — RWD with standard-range battery. AWD ($3,000 extra) and Extended-Range battery options available. NHTSA 5-star verified on earlier MY (carryover safety). IIHS not awarded TSP. Plug type CCS1 native with NACS adapter included for 2026.

notable_other: Heated front seats and heated steering wheel

trims.notes: Premium trim adds substantial comfort/luxury equipment over Select; same powertrain options available (RWD/AWD, Standard/Extended battery).

notable_other: Performance pedal kit and unique GT badging

trims.notes: GT trim is a performance flagship of the Mach-E lineup; same as the Mach-E Rally model in motor output but without Rally's lifted suspension and graphics package.

models.notes: Mustang Mach-E has three trims (Select, Premium, GT) plus the Mach-E Rally as a split-out separate model. All-electric crossover SUV. 2026 changes: NACS adapter standard for Tesla Supercharger access. Plug type remains CCS1 native; full native NACS expected later. Tow capacity 2,000 lb (Select), higher trims similar. Mach-E and Mach-E Rally share the same trim_family slug spaces but live in different models.

models.generation_context: Mustang Mach-E variant introduced for 2024 MY as a rally-inspired performance trim with lifted ride height; has its own ford.com page so treated as separate model per perf-variant rule. For 2026, the heritage rally graphics and rear spoiler became optional rather than standard.
models.model_summary: Off-pavement-oriented variant of the Mustang Mach-E with 1-inch suspension lift, all-terrain tires, underbody protective shields, and 480-hp dual-motor AWD powertrain. RallySport drive mode tuned for loose surfaces. Sole-trim model.

reliability.summary: No 2026 MY numeric reliability data; shares core architecture with Mustang Mach-E GT.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Mach-E Rally as a uniquely playful EV — the lifted suspension, all-terrain tires, and RallySport drive mode make it the first EV crossover engineered to do controlled slides on dirt. Trade-offs: range drops to ~255 miles, and the rally graphics polarize buyers (now optional for 2026).

owner_reviews.summary: Sample size too limited for 2026 MY Rally specifically. Owner sentiment for 2024-2025 Rally generally positive on driving feel.

notable_other: MagneRide adaptive dampers tuned for off-road
Unique grille shield with integrated LED fog lamps
RallySport drive mode (added for slide-friendly handling)
Heritage Mach-E Rally graphics now optional ($1,490 with rear spoiler)

trims.notes: Sole-trim model (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). Lifted-suspension off-road-capable variant of the Mach-E with the same 480-hp dual-motor powertrain as the Mach-E GT but with reduced range (255 mi vs 280 mi GT) due to lift, all-terrain tires, and additional weight.

models.notes: Mustang Mach-E Rally is a separate model from Mustang Mach-E (own ford.com /suvs/mach-e/models/rally/ page). Sole-trim model. New for 2026: rally graphics and rear spoiler now optional ($1,490 combined) instead of standard. NACS adapter included for Tesla Supercharger access.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Bronco Sport, introduced for 2021 MY on Ford's C2 platform (shared with Escape). Mid-cycle update for 2025+ added new front fascia, larger touchscreen, and revised trim equipment. 2026 carryover with minor pricing changes.
models.model_summary: Compact AWD-standard crossover SUV with rugged Bronco styling. Four trims (Big Bend, Heritage, Outer Banks, Badlands) with the Badlands using a more powerful 2.0L EcoBoost and the others using a 180-hp 1.5L EcoBoost. 8-speed automatic standard.

reliability.summary: Ford brand scored 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (below 204 industry avg). However, CR 2026 ranked Ford 11th overall with brand score 48/100 — Ford's best showing in 15 years — and CR specifically called out Ford Bronco Sport as scoring above-average. Earlier 2021-2023 Bronco Sport had recalls for fuel injector cracking; subsequent updates addressed.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Bronco Sport for its boxy styling, capable AWD system (especially in Badlands trim with twin-clutch rear differential), and surprisingly roomy interior for the segment. Common critiques include slower acceleration from the 1.5L engine, lower fuel economy than crossover rivals, and middling refinement on the highway.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the styling, off-road capability of the Badlands, and easy-to-load cargo area. Complaints include sluggish 1.5L acceleration, fuel economy below expectations, and infrequent but reported electrical/recall issues.

notable_other: Five G.O.A.T. (Goes Over Any Type of Terrain) drive modes

trims.notes: Base Bronco Sport trim. 1.5L EcoBoost three-cylinder turbo with 180 hp. Heritage trim shares same powertrain (styled differently).

notable_other: Heritage-specific paint colors (Robin's Egg Blue, Yellowstone Metallic)

trims.notes: Heritage shares mechanicals with Big Bend; differentiated by retro 1966-Bronco styling cues.
Outer Banks is the comfort-focused trim with the 1.5L powertrain.

notable_other: Twin-clutch rear drive unit with locking differential
Heated leather front seats and heated steering wheel
Steel skid plates and front tow hooks
Two additional G.O.A.T. drive modes (Mud/Ruts, Rock Crawl)
Higher 2,200-lb tow rating (with optional Class II Trailer Tow Package)

trims.notes: Badlands has its own engine/drivetrain calibration; counted as its own trim family due to unique exterior fascia.

models.notes: Bronco Sport has four trims (Big Bend / Heritage / Outer Banks / Badlands). Big Bend, Heritage, Outer Banks share 1.5L EcoBoost (180 hp); Badlands has 2.0L EcoBoost (250 hp). All AWD-standard. The Bronco Sport Sasquatch and additional packages are equipment packages per spec §6.5, not separate trims.

models.generation_context: 6th-gen Bronco, reintroduced for 2021 MY on Ford's body-on-frame T6 platform (shared with Ranger). 2026 MY brings the Wildtrak back as an equipment package on Badlands, plus the new Stroppe Edition (4-door only).
models.model_summary: Body-on-frame mid-size off-road SUV; available in 2-door and 4-door configurations with removable doors/roof. Six trims (Base, Big Bend, Black Diamond, Outer Banks, Badlands, Heritage Edition, Stroppe Edition) plus the Bronco Raptor split as separate model. Standard 2.3L EcoBoost I4 (300 hp) or optional 2.7L EcoBoost V6 (330 hp).

reliability.summary: No 2026 MY numeric reliability data published. Earlier 6th-gen Bronco had multiple recalls for hardtop water leaks, engine valve issues, and faulty rear-view cameras; many addressed via software/parts.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the Bronco's retro-modern styling, removable roof/doors, segment-leading off-road capability (Badlands+ trims with front sway bar disconnect), and breadth of trim choice. Critiques include polarizing on-road manners (especially with the Sasquatch package's 35-inch tires), and middling fuel economy.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners love the styling, off-road capability, and unique character. Common complaints: hardtop noise/wind, fuel economy below expectations, and quality issues with early production years.

notable_other: G.O.A.T. Modes with 5 drive modes (Normal, Eco, Sport, Slippery, Sand)

trims.notes: Base Bronco 2-door with 2.3L EcoBoost / 7-speed manual or 10-speed auto. 16-inch steel wheels. Limited ADAS features standard.
Modest comfort/convenience step up from Base. Same powertrain.

notable_other: Marine-grade vinyl seats and rubberized flooring (washable interior)

trims.notes: Off-road-prep trim focused on durability and easy cleaning.

notable_other: Heated leather front seats and steering wheel

trims.notes: Comfort/style-focused trim; primary alternative to off-road-focused trims.

notable_other: HOSS 2.0 suspension with Bilstein position-sensitive dampers
7 G.O.A.T. drive modes including Mud/Ruts, Rock Crawl, Baja
Locking front and rear differentials available
Wildtrak Package available ($11,945): 2.7L V6, HOSS 3.0 FOX bypass, Sasquatch, Black Appearance

trims.notes: Most off-road-capable production Bronco trim (besides Bronco Raptor). The Wildtrak is no longer a standalone trim in 2026; it returns as a $11,945 equipment package on Badlands. Available with 2.3L base engine but most buyers select 2.7L V6.

notable_other: White-painted roof, white grille with 'FORD' lettering
Heritage-exclusive paint colors (Robin's Egg Blue, etc.)

trims.notes: Heritage Edition celebrates the original Bronco's 60th anniversary; retro styling on Badlands-derived mechanicals.

notable_other: HOSS 3.0 suspension with FOX 3.1 internal-bypass dampers
Sasquatch Package standard (35-inch tires, 17-inch beadlock-capable wheels)
Code Orange interior accents and Stroppe Racing badging

trims.notes: Top non-Raptor Bronco trim. Singleton trim_family. Stroppe is unique badging package on Badlands-derived hardware with HOSS 3.0 vs Badlands' HOSS 2.0.

models.notes: Bronco has 7 trims for 2026 (Base / Big Bend / Black Diamond / Outer Banks / Badlands / Heritage Edition / Stroppe Edition). Wildtrak returns as a $11,945 package on Badlands (not a separate trim). Bronco Raptor is split as a separate model (its own ford.com page). All trims standard 4WD; 2-door or 4-door body styles. 2.3L EcoBoost (300 hp) standard on Base/Big Bend/Black Diamond/Outer Banks/Heritage; 2.7L EcoBoost V6 (330 hp) standard on Badlands/Stroppe and optional on others.

models.generation_context: Bronco Raptor introduced for 2022 MY as the high-performance off-road flagship of the Bronco lineup. Carryover for 2026 with minor pricing changes.
models.model_summary: Most off-road-capable production Bronco: 3.0L EcoBoost V6 (418 hp / 440 lb-ft), HOSS 4.0 suspension with FOX Live Valve dampers, 37-inch BFGoodrich tires on beadlock-capable wheels, and Baja drive mode. 4-door body only. Sole-trim model.

reliability.summary: Low-volume halo trim; no 2026 numeric reliability scores published. Shares core body and base engine architecture with standard Bronco.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Bronco Raptor as the most extreme off-road production SUV in the US market — Baja-rally-prepped suspension, wider track, 418-hp 3.0L V6, and 37-inch tires. Critiques: heavy curb weight (~5,800 lb), 15-mpg combined fuel economy, and stiffness on paved roads.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise extreme off-road competence and unique character. Common complaints: harsh on-road ride, poor fuel economy (high-priority off-roader trade-off), wide stance hard to park.

notable_other: 3.0L EcoBoost V6 (418 hp / 440 lb-ft) — Bronco-Raptor-only powertrain
HOSS 4.0 suspension with FOX Live Valve internal-bypass dampers (front 3.1, rear 3.1)
Widened track (8.6 inches wider than standard Bronco)
37-inch BFGoodrich KO2 all-terrain tires on 17-inch beadlock-capable wheels
Baja drive mode for high-speed off-road use
Code Orange exterior accents and Raptor-specific badging

trims.notes: Sole-trim model (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). 4-door body only. Significantly wider track than standard Bronco. Tow ratings increased to 4,500 lb.

models.notes: Bronco Raptor is a separate model from Bronco (own ford.com /suvs/bronco/models/raptor/ page). Sole-trim model. 4-door body only. Bronco-Raptor-specific 3.0L EcoBoost V6 (418 hp); HOSS 4.0 FOX Live Valve suspension; 37-inch tires.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Escape, introduced for 2020 MY; mid-cycle refresh for 2023 MY. 2026 is the final model year before discontinuation (Ford has not announced a successor). ICE, HEV, and PHEV powertrains continue.
models.model_summary: Compact crossover SUV offered in ICE, hybrid, and plug-in hybrid powertrains. Six trims: Active (ICE FWD/AWD), ST-Line (ICE), ST-Line Select Hybrid, ST-Line Elite Hybrid, Platinum Hybrid, and Plug-in Hybrid. 2026 is the Escape's final model year.

reliability.summary: Ford brand scored 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). However, CR 2026 ranked Ford 11th overall (48/100) — Ford's best showing in 15 years — and CR specifically called out Ford Escape Hybrid as scoring above-average. Earlier 4th-gen Escape (2020-2024) had recalls for fuel system, battery, and software issues; subsequent updates have improved reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Escape's smooth hybrid powertrain, comfortable ride, and large 13.2-inch touchscreen. Critiques: aging interior materials, modest cargo space vs rivals, and final-year status without a clear successor.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners like fuel economy of the hybrid versions and ride comfort. Complaints include software glitches, transmission roughness on the ICE, and quality issues vs Asian rivals.

notable_other: Ford Co-Pilot360 Assist+ standard ADAS suite

trims.notes: Base trim of ICE powertrain line. 1.5L EcoBoost FWD. AWD optional ($1,500).

notable_other: Black exterior accents and sport grille
ActiveX seat trim with red stitching

trims.notes: ICE ST-Line is sporty styling tier; mechanically same as Active.

notable_other: FWD standard; AWD available on higher trims

trims.notes: Base trim of Hybrid powertrain line (separate base per multi-powertrain rule). 2.5L Atkinson-cycle I4 + electric assist; 192 hp combined.
Platinum Hybrid is top-spec hybrid trim. AWD standard.
Sole-trim model of PHEV powertrain line. Per spec §3.6 v1.1, fuel_economy populated with charge-sustaining MPG (gas mode); ev_specifics.mpge_combined holds charge-depleting MPGe.

models.notes: Escape has three powertrain lines (ICE / Hybrid / Plug-in Hybrid), each with its own base trim per multi-powertrain rule: Active (ICE FWD), ST-Line Select Hybrid (HEV), Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV). Six total trims for 2026. 2026 is the final model year of the 4th-gen Escape.

models.generation_context: 6th-gen Explorer, introduced for 2020 MY; major mid-cycle refresh for 2025 MY with new interior tech, restyled grille/headlights, and revised trim lineup. 2026 MY carryover with new Tremor trim and minor pricing changes.
models.model_summary: Three-row family SUV. Six trims (Active 100A, Active, ST-Line, Tremor, Platinum, ST). Standard 2.3L EcoBoost I4 (300 hp); 3.0L EcoBoost V6 (400 hp) standard on ST and optional on Tremor/Platinum. RWD standard on lower trims, AWD on ST/Tremor.

reliability.summary: No 2026 MY numeric reliability data published. Prior gen Explorer had numerous recalls (2020-2023) for software, transmission, and door issues — many addressed. Consumer Reports rated 2024 Explorer below average.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 2025-2026 refresh for the improved interior tech (new 13.2-inch screen, Google Built-In) and family-friendly third row. Critiques: middling fuel economy across the lineup, on-road handling is less sporty than rivals, and reliability concerns linger from earlier MY recalls.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise spacious interior, modern tech, and V6 performance. Complaints involve software glitches, infotainment issues, and lower-than-expected fuel economy.

notable_other: Google Built-In navigation and Play store

trims.notes: Base 'Active 100A' trim ($38,465) is fleet-oriented base; 'Active' retail trim starts at $40,585. Using lower entry MSRP. AWD available ($1,895).

notable_other: 20-inch dark wheels and ST-Line sport styling
Heated ActiveX seats with red stitching

trims.notes: Sport-styled trim; same 2.3L powertrain as Active.

notable_other: Off-road suspension with retuned dampers and 1-inch lift
Standard AWD with Trail Turn Assist
Tremor-specific orange accents and unique grille

trims.notes: New for 2026. Off-road-focused with retuned suspension.

notable_other: Premium leather seating with second-row captain's chairs (6-seat)

trims.notes: Top luxury trim for Explorer; not as performance-focused as ST.

notable_other: 3.0L EcoBoost V6 (400 hp / 415 lb-ft) with sport tuning
ST sport seats with red stitching

trims.notes: Explorer ST kept as TRIM (not separate model) per ford.com showroom-page rule — although ST has its own /models/st/ page, it's listed alongside other trims on the main Explorer overview, so treated as performance trim within the Explorer model per spec §6.4.

models.notes: Explorer 6th-gen with 2025 mid-cycle refresh. Six trims (Active / Active 100A entry / ST-Line / Tremor / Platinum / ST). 2.3L EcoBoost I4 base; 3.0L EcoBoost V6 on Platinum/ST (optional Tremor). 7- or 6-seat configurations depending on trim. New Tremor trim for 2026. Active 100A is fleet-oriented entry; retail Active is $40,585.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Expedition, all-new for 2025 MY (introduced fall 2024). 2026 MY is the first full year of the new generation with carryover pricing and equipment. Available in standard wheelbase and extended-length MAX body styles.
models.model_summary: Full-size body-on-frame three-row SUV. Four trims (Active, Platinum, Tremor, King Ranch) with all standard-wheelbase trims (except Tremor) also offered in extended-length MAX body. Single 3.5L EcoBoost V6 with 400 hp (440 hp high-output for Tremor/King Ranch).

reliability.summary: All-new 5th-gen for 2025; no JD Power VDS data for new generation. Prior 4th-gen had varied reliability. Consumer Reports predicted-reliability for 2026 Expedition not yet published.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the all-new 2025-2026 Expedition for the modern interior design with massive 24-inch panoramic display, improved seating capacity (8 passengers standard), and the new Tremor trim's off-road capability. Critiques: heavy curb weight, fuel economy below Tahoe/Yukon, and pricing approaches near-luxury territory.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the new interior, BlueCruise, and capability. Common complaints involve high fuel costs and ride quality with the larger wheel options.

notable_other: All-new dash with 24-inch panoramic display option
Three-row seating for 8 (bench second row standard)

trims.notes: Base Active trim ($62,400) — fleet/retail-shared base; XL/work-spec at $57,400 is fleet-only. 4WD option ($3,000).
Mid-luxury Platinum trim; 7-seat configuration with captain's chairs.

notable_other: Underbody skid plates and front tow hooks
Unique grille and Tremor-specific orange accents
Tremor only in standard wheelbase (no MAX)

trims.notes: Off-road-focused Tremor available only in standard wheelbase (no MAX).

notable_other: Adaptive damping suspension with auto level
Saddle-brown 'King Ranch' leather upholstery with branded W badging
Heated/ventilated/massaging front seats and heated/cooled 2nd row

trims.notes: Top-luxury Expedition. MAX (extended length) adds $3,000 over standard wheelbase on King Ranch, Active, and Platinum.

models.notes: Expedition 5th-gen has four trims (Active / Platinum / Tremor / King Ranch). All-new for 2025 MY. MAX (extended-length) body adds $3,000 and is available on Active / Platinum / King Ranch (Tremor is standard-wheelbase only). High-output 3.5L EcoBoost V6 (440 hp) standard on Tremor and King Ranch; standard 3.5L (400 hp) on Active and Platinum.

models.generation_context: 1st-gen Maverick, introduced for 2022 MY; mid-cycle refresh for 2025 MY added new 13.2-inch screen and revised trim equipment. 2026 MY is MotorTrend's Truck of the Year and adds the new Lobo street-performance trim.
models.model_summary: Compact unibody pickup with 4.5-foot bed. Five trims (XL, XLT, Lariat, Lobo, Tremor). 2.5L Atkinson hybrid (191 hp) standard on XL/XLT/Lariat (FWD only); 2.0L EcoBoost (250 hp) optional and standard on Lobo/Tremor (AWD). Lobo is new street-performance trim with advanced 4WD, 7-speed quick-shift, and paddle shifters.

reliability.summary: Ford brand scored 228 PP100 in JD Power 2026 VDS (industry avg 204). However, CR 2026 ranked Ford 11th overall (48/100) — Ford's best showing in 15 years — and CR specifically called out Ford Maverick as scoring above-average. Earlier Maverick had recalls (2022-2024) for fuel-pump connector, taillight wiring, and rear camera; subsequent updates addressed.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently call the Maverick the most practical truck under $30k — hybrid efficiency, useful 4.5-ft bed, and approachable size. MotorTrend named the 2026 Maverick its Truck of the Year. Critiques: small footprint limits towing/payload compared to Ranger; Lobo's hot-hatch styling polarizing for some traditional truck buyers.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners love the practicality, fuel economy of the hybrid, and value. Common complaints involve recall fatigue, limited cargo box width, and limited rear-seat space.

trims.notes: Base XL trim — hybrid standard, FWD. Trucks use bed_length_in/bed_volume_cuft schema extension per spec; bed_length_in=54 (4.5 ft), bed_volume_cuft=33.3.

notable_other: Available Co-Pilot360 Assist+ with adaptive cruise

trims.notes: Comfort-focused step up from XL with same hybrid powertrain.
Top hybrid trim; 2.0L EcoBoost AWD optional ($3,305).

notable_other: Lobo-specific 2.0L EcoBoost with 7-speed quick-shift trans (250 hp, paddle shifters)

trims.notes: Lobo is new for MY2025 — kept as TRIM within Maverick per ford.com structure (no separate showroom model page). Street-performance trim. Lobo and Tremor share the EcoBoost engine but have different chassis tuning.

notable_other: Tremor off-road suspension with longer travel (1" higher)
Twin-clutch rear drive unit with locking differential
Steel front skid plate and tow hooks
Six G.O.A.T. drive modes incl. Mud/Ruts and Sand

trims.notes: Off-road-focused Maverick. Tremor kept as TRIM within Maverick per ford.com structure (no separate model page).

models.notes: Maverick has 5 trims (XL / XLT / Lariat / Lobo / Tremor). Two powertrain lines: hybrid (XL/XLT/Lariat standard, FWD) and 2.0L EcoBoost (Lobo/Tremor standard with AWD, optional on Lariat). XL is base of hybrid line (lowest MSRP and primary base). Lobo and Tremor are kept as TRIMS (no separate ford.com showroom page). 2026 won MotorTrend Truck of the Year. All trims SuperCrew 4-door with 4.5-foot bed. Trucks use bed_length_in/bed_volume_cuft schema extension per spec §3.8.

models.generation_context: 5th-gen Ranger, introduced for 2024 MY on global T6.2 platform. SuperCrew 4-door body only. 2026 MY carryover with minor pricing changes.
models.model_summary: Midsize body-on-frame pickup with SuperCrew 4-door cab and 5-foot bed. Three trims (XL, XLT, Lariat). Standard 2.3L EcoBoost I4 (270 hp); optional 2.7L EcoBoost V6 (315 hp) on Lariat. RWD standard, 4WD ~$3,500 option. Ranger Raptor sold as separate model.

reliability.summary: Limited 2026 data; 2024-2025 new-gen Ranger had moderate teething issues; Consumer Reports rated 2025 Ranger average in predicted reliability.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the Ranger's improved ride quality, 5-foot bed flexibility, optional 2.7L V6, and big 12-inch screen. Critiques: limited engine options (no diesel for US), towing/payload below class leaders, and only SuperCrew body sold in US.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise ride comfort and big screen tech. Common complaints: lower fuel economy vs hybrid Maverick, awkward seating ergonomics for taller drivers.

trims.notes: Base Ranger trim. 2.3L EcoBoost I4 RWD. 4WD optional ($3,500). All US Rangers SuperCrew with 5-foot bed.
Volume-seller trim with tech and comfort upgrades over XL.

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and wireless charging
B&O 10-speaker premium audio with HD Radio

trims.notes: Top Ranger trim (except Raptor). 2.7L V6 optional for buyers wanting more power.

models.notes: Ranger 5th-gen has three trims (XL / XLT / Lariat) plus Ranger Raptor split as separate model. All SuperCrew 4-door body with 5-foot bed. RWD standard; 4WD optional. 2.3L EcoBoost I4 standard; 2.7L EcoBoost V6 optional on Lariat.

models.generation_context: Ranger Raptor introduced to US for 2024 MY (long sold overseas before). 2026 MY carryover with minor pricing changes.
models.model_summary: Performance off-road variant of the Ranger. 3.0L EcoBoost V6 HO (405 hp / 430 lb-ft), Fox 2.5-inch Live Valve internal-bypass dampers, locking front and rear differentials, 33-inch BFGoodrich KO2 all-terrain tires. Sole-trim model.

reliability.summary: Limited US-market data for new 2024+ Ranger Raptor.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Ranger Raptor as the most fun midsize off-road truck — Fox Live Valve dampers, twin lockers, and a willing-to-rev 3.0L V6 deliver Baja-style capability at a lower price than the F-150 Raptor. Critiques: fuel economy (17 combined), wide stance harder for city use, payload reduced vs standard Ranger.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise off-road capability, V6 sound, and unique character. Common complaint: low payload (1,400 lb).

notable_other: Raptor-exclusive 3.0L EcoBoost V6 HO (405 hp / 430 lb-ft)
Seven drive modes incl. Baja, Rock Crawl
Aluminum front and rear bash plates

trims.notes: Sole-trim model (atomic rule: is_base_trim true + delta_from_base null). 4WD-only, no other configurations. Wider track and aggressive widebody bodywork than standard Ranger.

models.notes: Ranger Raptor is a separate model from Ranger (own ford.com /trucks/ranger/models/raptor/ page). Sole-trim model. Raptor-exclusive 3.0L EcoBoost V6 HO; Fox Live Valve suspension.

models.generation_context: 14th-gen F-150, introduced for 2021 MY; mid-cycle refresh for 2024 MY with new front fascia, larger screens, and updated trim equipment. 2026 carryover with new 4x2 drivetrain options on Lariat/King Ranch/Platinum to lower entry prices.
models.model_summary: America's best-selling full-size pickup. Eight regular trims (XL, STX, XLT, Lariat, King Ranch, Platinum, Tremor) plus separate Raptor and Raptor R models. Multiple powertrains: 2.7L EcoBoost V6 (325 hp), 3.5L EcoBoost V6 (400 hp), 5.0L V8 (400 hp), 3.5L PowerBoost Hybrid (430 hp). Cab options: Regular, SuperCab, SuperCrew. Bed options: 5.5', 6.5', 8'.

reliability.jd_power_vds_source: brand-level (F-150 ranked 3rd in full-size light-duty pickup segment in 2026 VDS)
reliability.summary: Ford F-150 ranked 3rd in full-size light-duty pickup segment in the JD Power 2026 Vehicle Dependability Study, behind Ram 1500 and Chevy Silverado. Ford brand scored 228 PP100 (well below 204 industry avg). CR 2026 ranked Ford 11th overall with brand score 48/100 — Ford's best showing in 15 years, with F-150 Hybrid specifically scoring above-average. PowerBoost hybrid system has had some reliability complaints.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rank F-150 atop or near the top of full-size pickup comparisons. Praise: huge breadth of powertrains (V8 + hybrid + EcoBoost), best-in-class towing on Max Trailer Tow Package, BlueCruise hands-free driving, and Pro Power Onboard generator. Critiques: ride quality varies dramatically by trim; aftermarket recall history. F-150 Lightning and Raptor variants reviewed separately.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise versatility, powertrain choice, and ride. Complaints involve recall history, quality control on early-production trucks, and software glitches.

notable_other: Regular Cab, SuperCab, and SuperCrew bodies available

trims.notes: Base XL trim. 2.7L EcoBoost V6 standard; 5.0L V8 and 3.5L EcoBoost optional. Various cab/bed combinations available. F-150 uses bed_length_in/bed_volume_cuft schema extension per spec.
STX is sport-styled appearance package on XL platform.
Volume mid-trim. Heart of F-150 sales mix.

notable_other: Heated steering wheel and memory driver seat

trims.notes: Lariat is gateway to luxury F-150 trims.
Off-road-focused F-150 below the Raptor models.

notable_other: Saddle-brown King Ranch branded leather upholstery

trims.notes: King Ranch is luxury-Western F-150. 4x4 default; new 4x2 option for 2026 saves ~$4,000.

notable_other: Multi-contour seats with Active Motion massage

trims.notes: Highest non-Raptor F-150 trim. Tech-focused luxury versus King Ranch's Western theme.

notable_other: 3.5L EcoBoost V6 + 35-kW electric motor (430 hp combined)
Available across Lariat / King Ranch / Platinum trims

trims.notes: Base trim of Hybrid powertrain line. PowerBoost Hybrid is an optional upgrade on Lariat/King Ranch/Platinum (priced from Lariat trim level here). Industry-leading 7.2kW Pro Power Onboard.

models.notes: F-150 has 8 main trims (XL / STX / XLT / Lariat / King Ranch / Platinum / Tremor) plus PowerBoost Hybrid as a powertrain line (Lariat/King Ranch/Platinum). Two powertrain lines: ICE (2.7L/3.5L/5.0L) and PowerBoost Hybrid. Multiple body and bed configurations. F-150 Raptor and F-150 Raptor R sold as separate models. F-150 Lightning EV sold as separate model. New for 2026: 4x2 drivetrain options on Lariat/King Ranch/Platinum lowering entry pricing by ~$4,000.

models.generation_context: 3rd-gen F-150 Raptor, refreshed for 2024 MY with new HOSS 5.0 suspension and 720-Watt 18-speaker B&O Unleashed audio. Carryover for 2026 with new option packages.
models.model_summary: High-speed off-road performance variant of F-150. 3.5L EcoBoost High-Output V6 (450 hp / 510 lb-ft), Fox Live Valve internal-bypass dampers, locking front and rear differentials, 37-inch BFGoodrich KO2 tires. SuperCrew 4-door body, 5.5-foot bed only. Sole-trim model.

reliability.summary: Limited data; shared 3.5L EcoBoost HO architecture with prior Raptors is well-known.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers continue to praise the F-150 Raptor as the benchmark for high-speed desert-running production trucks. HOSS 5.0 suspension upgrade for 2024 improved damping and bump absorption. Critiques: now significantly outpriced by Raptor R V8; fuel economy (~16 combined).

owner_reviews.summary: Owners universally praise the off-road capability and unique character. Top complaint: fuel economy and curb weight on pavement.

notable_other: 3.5L EcoBoost High-Output V6 (450 hp / 510 lb-ft) — Raptor-only
37-inch BFGoodrich KO2 tires on 17-inch beadlock-capable wheels

trims.notes: Sole-trim model. SuperCrew 4-door with 5.5-foot bed; 4WD only. 37-inch tires standard. New for 2024 was HOSS 5.0 with FOX Live Valve dampers.

models.notes: F-150 Raptor is a separate model from F-150 (own /trucks/f150/models/raptor/ page). Sole-trim model. Available as 35-inch tire 'Standard Raptor' or 37-inch 'Raptor 37' package; treated as one trim with 37-inch as standard config here.

models.generation_context: F-150 Raptor R introduced for 2023 MY as the most powerful Ford pickup ever. Carryover for 2026 with minor equipment updates.
models.model_summary: Most powerful production F-150 ever. 5.2L supercharged Predator V8 (720 hp / 640 lb-ft) — shared with Mustang GTD and Dark Horse SC. HOSS 5.1 suspension with Fox 3.1 Live Valve dampers, 37-inch BFGoodrich KO2 tires. SuperCrew with 5.5-foot bed. Sole-trim model.

reliability.summary: Limited 2026 data; supercharged Predator V8 is a low-volume halo engine.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Raptor R as the most outrageous production pickup — 720-hp supercharged V8 in a Baja-tuned chassis. Outpaces some sports cars in 0-60 (4.4 sec) while still capable of high-speed desert running. Critiques: 12-mpg combined, $110k+ price approaches Lamborghini Urus territory.

owner_reviews.summary: Sample size insufficient for 2026 specifically. Earlier MY Raptor R owners praise V8 sound and acceleration; complaints universal on fuel economy.

notable_other: HOSS 5.1 suspension with Fox 3.1 Live Valve dampers
Raptor R-specific interior with carbon-fiber accents

trims.notes: Sole-trim model. Most powerful production F-150 ever. SuperCrew with 5.5-foot bed; 4WD only.

models.notes: F-150 Raptor R is a separate model from F-150 and F-150 Raptor (own /trucks/f150/models/raptor-r/ page). Sole-trim model. 5.2L supercharged V8 (Predator) shared with Mustang GTD.

models.generation_context: F-150 Lightning electric pickup introduced for 2022 MY. 2026 MY drops the standard-range battery (98 kWh) entirely — all trims now use the 123 kWh Extended Range battery. XLT trim replaced by new STX. Pricing reduced across most trims for 2026.
models.model_summary: Fully electric F-150 pickup. Five trims (Pro, STX, Flash, Lariat, Platinum). 123 kWh Extended Range battery standard across all trims for 2026 (up from 98 kWh on Pro/STX in 2025). Dual-motor AWD-electric standard; 536-hp output. Pro is fleet-only, retail starts at STX.

reliability.summary: No 2026 MY numeric data. Lightning had several high-voltage battery and charging-system recalls in 2022-2024; Consumer Reports rated 2025 average.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise Lightning's truck capability with EV efficiency, massive frunk (14.1 cu ft), and Pro Power Onboard 9.6kW backup-power generator. Critiques: real-world towing range significantly less than rated; charging speed (~150 kW peak) is now slower than Tesla Cybertruck and competitors; price still high vs gas F-150.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the silent power, instant torque, and Pro Power Onboard generator capability. Complaints: range degradation while towing, slower DC fast charging than rivals, and recall history.

notable_other: 14.1 cu ft frunk with cooler drain and outlets
Intelligent Backup Power (home backup via Sunrun)

trims.notes: Retail-base trim for 2026 (Pro is fleet-only $54,780). STX replaces XLT for 2026. EPA published 2026 entries pending — fuel_economy.mpge_combined per Ford/EPA manufacturer claim, source set to brand model-browse page per §4 fallback. NHTSA 5-star carryover from 2025.
Tech-upgraded volume trim. Same 123 kWh battery and 580-hp dual-motor as STX.

notable_other: Heated/ventilated leather front seats with memory

trims.notes: Luxury mid-trim. $2,000 price cut for 2026 vs 2025.

notable_other: Multi-contour Active Motion massaging front seats

trims.notes: Top luxury Lightning trim. Same powertrain across all Lightning trims for 2026.

models.notes: F-150 Lightning is a separate model from F-150 (own ford.com /trucks/f150-lightning/ page). All-electric pickup. Four retail trims (STX / Flash / Lariat / Platinum); Pro is fleet-only at $54,780. New for 2026: 123 kWh battery now standard across all trims; XLT replaced by STX as entry retail; price cuts of $2,000-$4,000 on Flash/Lariat. NACS adapter included for Tesla Supercharger access. EPA-specific 2026 figures pending publication for individual trims — using Ford-published manufacturer figures from press release; source set to fueleconomy.gov 2026 Ford brand-browse page.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Super Duty, introduced for 2023 MY. 2026 MY carryover with minor equipment updates. F-250 sits between F-150 and F-350 in payload/towing capacity.
models.model_summary: Heavy-duty 3/4-ton full-size pickup. Six trims (XL, XLT, Lariat, King Ranch, Platinum, Limited). Three engines: 6.8L gas V8 (405 hp), 7.3L gas V8 (430 hp), or 6.7L Power Stroke diesel V8 (475 hp / 1,050 lb-ft) and high-output 6.7L diesel (500 hp / 1,200 lb-ft). Regular cab, SuperCab, and Crew Cab. Single-rear-wheel only.

reliability.summary: Limited 2026 numeric data. HD trucks rarely get full reliability surveys. Prior gen had varied feedback; 6.7L Power Stroke has long service history but new H.O. variant is less proven.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the F-250 Super Duty's massive towing capability, broad engine choice (gas + diesel), and refined interior on Platinum/Limited trims. Critiques: ride quality on empty bed, $90k+ pricing on Limited approaches luxury car territory.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise towing capability and powertrain choices. Common complaints: pricing on top trims, fuel consumption, ride quality empty.

notable_other: Optional 7.3L gas V8 ($2,000) or 6.7L Power Stroke diesel (~$11,000)
Regular Cab, SuperCab, Crew Cab configurations

trims.notes: Base F-250 XL. HD truck (GVWR >8500 lb) — no EPA MPG ratings, no NHTSA/IIHS ratings. Multiple cab/bed configurations from $45,675 (base RegCab). Diesel adds ~$11k.
Volume-seller XLT trim.

notable_other: Wireless charging and memory driver seat

trims.notes: Mid-luxury Lariat trim; common diesel pairing.

notable_other: Saddle-brown King Ranch leather upholstery with branded W
Massaging front seats and heated rear seats

trims.notes: Top-tier Western-luxury F-250 trim.

notable_other: Premium leather seating with multi-contour massaging seats

trims.notes: Tech-focused Platinum vs Western-themed King Ranch.

notable_other: 6.7L High-Output Power Stroke diesel standard (500 hp / 1,200 lb-ft)
Premium leather with quilted detail and unique stitching
Adaptive cruise with stop-and-go and BlueCruise
Heated/cooled massaging front seats with memory

trims.notes: Top luxury Super Duty trim. Diesel standard.

models.notes: F-250 Super Duty has six trims (XL / XLT / Lariat / King Ranch / Platinum / Limited). HD truck (GVWR >8500 lb) so NO EPA MPG ratings and NO NHTSA/IIHS ratings. Three engine options: 6.8L gas V8 (405 hp standard), 7.3L gas V8 (430 hp), 6.7L Power Stroke diesel (475 hp/1,050 lb-ft), 6.7L HO diesel (500 hp/1,200 lb-ft). Regular/Super/Crew cab, 6.75' or 8' bed. Tremor Package available across trims (~$3,500 option) per spec §6.5.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Super Duty F-350, introduced for 2023 MY. 2026 MY carryover with minor equipment updates. F-350 offers higher payload/towing than F-250 and the option of dual rear wheels (DRW).
models.model_summary: 1-ton heavy-duty pickup with single-rear-wheel (SRW) or dual-rear-wheel (DRW) configurations. Six trims (XL, XLT, Lariat, King Ranch, Platinum, Limited). Same engine choices as F-250 but with higher payload capacity (up to 8,000 lb) and towing (35,750 lb max with 5th wheel and DRW).

reliability.summary: Limited HD truck reliability data. Most F-350 use diesel engines with proven service history.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise F-350 capability ceiling (35,750-lb 5th-wheel towing). Critiques focus on price escalation at the top trims and reduced agility versus F-250.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise towing capability and durability; complaints similar to F-250 (price, fuel use).

notable_other: Higher payload and towing vs F-250
Up to 35,750-lb 5th-wheel towing with DRW + HO diesel

trims.notes: Base F-350 XL. No EPA MPG, no NHTSA/IIHS (HD truck). Numerous cab/bed/SRW/DRW configurations; XL Crew Cab DRW Long Bed starts ~$53k.

notable_other: 6.7L HO Power Stroke diesel standard (500 hp / 1,200 lb-ft)
Up to 35,750-lb 5th-wheel towing with DRW + HO diesel

trims.notes: Top F-350 trim. Other intermediate trims (XLT/Lariat/King Ranch/Platinum) priced between XL and Limited; price scales similar to F-250 range with $4k-$5k premium for F-350 spec.

models.notes: F-350 Super Duty has six trims (XL / XLT / Lariat / King Ranch / Platinum / Limited). Two representative trims captured (XL base + Limited top); intermediate trims similar to F-250 with $4-5k premium per trim. HD truck — no EPA MPG, no NHTSA/IIHS. SRW or DRW (dual-rear-wheel) configurations available; DRW required for max towing. Up to 35,750-lb 5th-wheel towing with HO diesel + DRW.

models.generation_context: 4th-gen Super Duty F-450, introduced for 2023 MY. 2026 MY carryover. F-450 is dual-rear-wheel only and primarily marketed as the maximum-capability tow rig.
models.model_summary: Maximum-capacity 1-ton-plus heavy-duty pickup. Dual-rear-wheel (DRW) only. Five trims (XL, XLT, Lariat, King Ranch, Platinum, Limited — same as F-250/F-350). 6.7L HO Power Stroke diesel standard on most trims. Crew Cab only. Up to 40,000-lb gooseneck and 7,260-lb payload.

reliability.summary: Limited HD truck reliability data published. Mostly fleet/commercial use.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has not yet been published as of research date 2026-05-15 (APEAL typically releases July).

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe F-450 as the maximum-capability production pickup in the US — 40,000-lb gooseneck towing rating. Critiques: very limited usability for non-towing duty, $100k+ pricing on luxury trims.

owner_reviews.summary: Sample sizes very limited (low-volume HD truck). Owners predominantly commercial/fleet.

notable_other: 6.7L Power Stroke diesel standard (475 hp / 1,050 lb-ft)

trims.notes: Base F-450. Diesel standard. DRW (dual-rear-wheel) standard. No EPA MPG, no NHTSA/IIHS (HD truck >8500 GVWR).

notable_other: 6.7L HO Power Stroke diesel standard (500 hp / 1,200 lb-ft)

trims.notes: Top luxury F-450 trim. Intermediate trims (XLT/Lariat/King Ranch/Platinum) priced between XL and Limited similar to F-350 spread.

models.notes: F-450 Super Duty has six trims (XL / XLT / Lariat / King Ranch / Platinum / Limited). Two representative trims captured. Dual-rear-wheel (DRW) and Crew Cab only. 6.7L Power Stroke diesel standard on most trims. HD truck — no EPA MPG, no NHTSA/IIHS ratings. Max 40,000-lb gooseneck towing, 7,260-lb payload.
